rootCause: Penpot MCP server-side 30 s execute_code budget. Per-board content batches each created ~150–270 shapes and exceeded 30 s. The plugin's in-memory state after each call showed the new shapes as present, but Penpot's subsequent server sync rolled the timed-out transactions back, leaving only the shell shapes that committed in an earlier session.

durableStrategyForResume.per_board_split: 3–4 small execute_code calls per board, ~40–60 shapes per call
durableStrategyForResume.per_call_budget: 5–15 s, well under 30 s server limit
durableStrategyForResume.final_cross_board_verify: re-check all 11 board child counts in one tiny probe to confirm nothing rolled back

blockerReason: Penpot server rolls back transactions whose execute_code response timed out. Per-board batches exceeded the 30 s budget. Requires retry with smaller per-board batches.
manualActionsRequested: Decide: (1) authorize same-session resume with smaller-batch strategy (send 'Penpot continue'), or (2) accept current state of 3 finished portfolio screens, or (3) pivot to HTML implementation now.
nextSafeStep: Operator authorizes one of Options 1/2/3 (see report). Implementer awaits trigger.

figmaFileMoveAndScreenshot.note: Figma parked; Penpot is the active design tool.
